title: Shinjis sense of self and intelligence
display_title: Shinjis sense of self and intelligence
wikitext:
[]: Shinji's sense of self and intelligence             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sun Jun 07, 2015 5:26 pm Do you guys and gals think Shinji is capable of psychoanalyzing himself and realizing how damaged he is before the hospital and Instrumentality incident would have happened? I know Anno wanted the self realization and psychoanalysis to happen during Instrumentality but would Shinji be able recognize that he needs psychological help?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Jun 07, 2015 5:31 pm I would say not. It's a difficult step to take in real life, even with people trying to advise one, so I think that without guidance a traumatised 14yo is not going to get to that level of self-awareness, not beyond the "I'm so fucked up" that we see. Oh, and checking the title you gave this, it's not really a matter of intelligence, but of acceptance.

[]:             Quote Post by ChaddyManPrime » Sun Jun 07, 2015 5:51 pm ^ I've personally felt that it takes intelligence to realize some behavior isn't normal in a human unless they are damaged.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sun Jun 07, 2015 6:40 pm Sure; what I meant is that it's not enough.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sun Jun 07, 2015 7:05 pm I think as early as ep4 he knew he was screwed up, but he forgot for a while when things were looking up.

[]:             Quote Post by Shoujo Kakumei Asuka » Sun Jun 07, 2015 10:08 pm I think that the question of whether he was capable or not is rather moot in the context of what happened in the series. At any rate, if you believe the two endings are separate continuities (like I do) then Shinji DID come to a realization in one instance. Even so, self-realization is a process of reaching out to people as well as developing healthier habits over a period of time. It also typically involves counseling as that makes the whole process a lot easier. It's not a sudden epiphany, and progress towards better mental health is often subject to setbacks. There also has to be a realization that even though you'll overcome your worst aspects, you'll never solve the problems on your own as well. So no, I don't think it would have happened before Instrumentality. Both because of how he can push away others as well as the fucked up setting he was in. If either of these were different, things would have been much better for Shinji. pwhodges wrote: I would say not. It's a difficult step to take in real life, even with people trying to advise one, so I think that without guidance a traumatised 14yo is not going to get to that level of self-awareness, not beyond the "I'm so fucked up" that we see.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Jun 08, 2015 2:50 pm Shoujo Kakumei Asuka wrote: I'll say it again. NERV needs psychologists. Of course that would have ruined the series we got, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Ispellnogood » Fri Jun 26, 2015 10:14 am The paradigm has been to blame it on weakness in personal character and the like. (Sick in the head? Well, it's your own damn fault, so flog yourself with a healthy helping of shame and work harder.
